column2: The formula is JobGroup's id modulo by 7. E.g: Merchant's jobgroup is 1, modulo 7 gives 1, so it is Monday
Another example: Cook's jobgroup is 7, modulo 7 gives 0, so it will appear on Sunday
Orchard with 1 or 2 Double beds, no indoor facility, residents should have INT between 120 and 149 for Apple production
Upgrade Monster Feed a little bit but not too much if you want to farm zoo animal in late game

(higher level Monster Feed will spawn more animals, but higher level will take long time to finish so if you want to capture animals for zoo, it might take long time to spawn another zoo animal)
Furthermore, don't deploy Monster Feed on Wasteland, check the data sheet for Cow/Pig spawn location
Chicken gives less Food than Pig and Pig gives less food than Cow
( check Monster tab, id 124-130 for Chicken/Pig/Cow information)
Lots of residents with Rancher skill equipped + high level Monster Feed
1) Calculate crit_rate:

column3: base crit rate followed a well defined table in function of Attacker's luck:

column6: at 0 luck, unmodified crit rate is 10%, at 99, it is 20%

column3: then if this attack is a skill, there is a modifier: crit_rate * (100 + skill's value) / 100 , skill's value is taken from value column in Skill's tab
then roll a number between 0 and 100 to see if atk is critical

column2: 2) if crit miss, calculate hit_rate, else go to 3

column3: Hit rate = 150 + attackerDexterity - defenderAgility / 5 - defenderLuck / 5 , capped between 5 and 97 ( min =5, max=97) , Agility is called Speed in game
then if this attack is a skill, there is a modifier: hit_rate * (100 - skill's value) / 100 , skill's value is taken from value column in Skill's tab
roll dice 1d100, if less than hit_rate then it is a hit

column2: 3) Calculate damage

column3: Magic attack is only skill with type column == 1 in Skill tab (only Fire, Ice, Lightning skills at the moment)
Atk's attack atk_Atk = INT if magic attack else ATK stat, Monster doesn't have INT, its INT is calculated as monster' Dex/2
Defender'defense def_Def = DEF stat, if it is under Armor breaker's effect (DEF Down), its def_Def = def_Def * (100 - skill's value) / 100
if critical, defender's defense is further modified (devided by 8): def_Def = def_Def / 8
damage = atk_Atk * random(80,120) / 100 , minimum = 1
damage = damage - def_Def * random (70,90) /100
if damage <=5: damage = random (1,10)
if weapon has advantage on that particular map's spot, damage = damage * 1.5

column2: exp gained depends on defeated monster, each monster provides different kind of exp on particular stats. Check "exps" column on "Monster" tab
Once defeated, system will choose randomly one stat between multiple stat that monster provides exp
Then exp gain is: 30 * multiplier * monster's level /100, with multiplier is value of chosen exp stat above
This will further be modified by other bonus like Exp I, II, III skills
given raw exp by killing monster (check exps column in Monster tab)
for each skill Exp UP: exp = exp * ( skill_value + 100 ) / 100; round down after ech calculation
so unit with 3 skills: exp ~ exp * 1.25 * 1.5 *1.75 ~ 3.28 times

column1: Decision to cast skills for each turn in battle
column2:
- Search for skill from 1st slot to last slot:
  + check if skill is useable:
    * to decide if skill useable: it checks skill's category, skill's type and skill's weapons:
            If if is activity skill category, then nope.
            If not enough MP then nope.
            If it is attack skill and skill has weapon's type then check equipped weapon, it it doesn't match then nope. AA3 doesn't weapon's type so Bow or not doesn't matter.
            If it is recovery skill then check current user's HP if it maxed then nope.
            If it is revival skill then check unit's HP, if it is bigger than 0 then nope.
           if skill is invoking then nope
            if it is neither Line Attack, Area Attack or Debuff then YES
           else if it is in front row: check if there is any one in skill's range (skill's range from Skill tab), if noone then nope, else YES
           else: NOPE 
  + if skill is useable then throw dice to know if it can be casted, if not, go to next slot <= slot in first position got better chance to be casted than last position

So only Line Attack, Area Attack and Debuff need to be in front line, any thing else could be done from range

unit's speed decideds the unit's attack frame, which is around 30 * (speed / 25) ^ (-0.362), the higher speed is, the lower attack frame will be, so unit will do action faster
base on this formula, unit at 6000 speed ( doable for 999 level unit) will attack around two times faster than unit at 1000 speed

You see the effect better with Legendary Cave where time gauge is stopped during attack action, till monster level 1000, monsters have around 600-1000 speed, and if your royal has 5000-6000 speed, your Royal will attack at least two times.
Give birth is decided at the moment of clicking on the crib, not the moment of clicking on DayCare center later
0) The compatibility showed on Date spot (A,B,C,D,E) is calculated: rank = (affinity - 50) / 10, round down,  which gives 1.. 5 corresponds to E .. A

column3: This gives a general idea about affinity, but the exact malus must be calculated with the formula below. Because lower than A compatibility may match two affinities, e.g: B rank compatibility can have affinity of 90 and 95 with different malus

Awakening is calculated from formula:  (father's awakening + 1 + mother's awakening + 1 + bonus_awakening) * affinity / 100, round down
   affinity is defaulted to 50 if there is no affinity in JobPair tab in data sheet, there are several ones in sheet: 60, 65, 70, 75, 80, 90, 95, 100
   bonus awakening is: same rank bonus, if it is D then bonus = -1  ... 3 for S. if mismatch, that is a -2 to awakening
   so effectively the malus of bad affinity is the affinity / 100, with the bad effect of round down
  (e.g  awakening for S couple 14 and  14 awakening with 100% affinity is : 100 * (14 + 1 + 14 + 1 + 3) /100 = 33 )
  for S couple 14 and 14 with 60 affinity (e.g: Royal with Samurai, E compatibility): 60 * ( 14+1+14+1+3)/100 = 19.8, round down to 19
column12:
The exact formula for awakening is calculated as: Affinity * ( father's awakening + mother's awakening + 2 + bonus_awakening) / 100 , rounded down
Affinity is an internal parameter between two job, for A compatibility, it is 100, for B, it can be 90 or 95 depends on job pair ... 60 or 65 for E.
Bonus_awakening is same rank bonus (-1 for a pair of D ... 3 for a pair of S), if mismatch, that is a -2.
So for a couple of S with A compatibility with 15 and 14 awakening, that is 100 * ( 14 + 15 + 2 + 3) /100 = 33
for a couple of S with E compatibility with 14 and 14 awakening, supposed 60 affinity, that is 60 * (14 + 15 +2 +3) / 100 = 19.8, round down to 19 awakening.
Because of multiplication/division, the compatibility penalty isn't a fixed value.

column3: Gender is random
stat is also random based on awakening, and there are 3 random sets, 1 for choosing father job, 1 for mother job and one for pair job

column2: egg_rank = defeated monster's level * random(80,120) / 100 / random (8,12), round down after each division, capped between 1 and 500. So for monster 3210, max egg rank is 3210 * 120 / 8 / 100 ~ 481
each egg has a level (so if it is hatched, that will be pet's level too): egg's level is random between 1 and egg's rank/2
given min, max hatch basis column in Monster's tab and current_value in that monster's category (Atk, Def, Balance, Special)
value = (current_value - min) / (max -min) * 100
if value < 0: none
if value < 30 : low possibility, rate = 5
if 30 <= value < 85 : medium possibility, rate = 30
if 85<=value <= 100: high possibility, rate = 60
if value > 100: low possibility, rate =5
the reason why residents comes back to battle with only 1 HP:
1) Search for HP recovery facilities
2) Get the one highest priority facility in list <= I didnt check if it omits already occupied facility
3) Try to assign to that facility, if it fails => abort

order depends on two factor:

- priority, firstly lookup from internal table and then modified by skill (backup +4, daring charge+2), and by additional param OwnerPlayer (??) +2
- and DEF stat.

I didn't check how these two factor play together to give exact idea how a pawn be placed in front or back, but I supposed that even with backup, if unit has his DEF high enough to compensate the priority, he will still be put in front

1 in-game day: it has the same system with hour, seconds as in-real-life

AT 6AM, it is considered early morning

7AM: morning

8AM- 3PM: daytime

4PM-6PM: evening

7PM-8PM: night

9PM-5PM: midnight

Sleep time is between 8PM-5PM (included). If there is a decision loop falling inside this time, they will go to sleep.

The base time for crafting is 4 in-game hour per day. With Skilled Craftsman II, it is extended to 8 hour, if you excluded lag time and various decision time, they don't have much time to do anything else. Unless they finish their craft (no more shelves to fill), then their craft time is finished, no more check till next day.

If I get it right, the skill with station (mining, wagon cart, shop's station) get priority over other skills

column1: Farming Chaos stones,  e.g: 1 ninja awakening 14 on 6 chaos stones level 3200: https://youtu.be/7cwHFVK6kPs

column9: Blue: The tile where no unit/monster can stand on (they can walk by, but in no way they can stay there), e.g: tile with sign board or cave or building (Trading Post for example)
White: Tiles where unit/monster can stand on
AI will search for a nearby tile they can stay in a radius of 3-4 tiles around them, so they will walk there next
As the only space available is the closed space at the center, they will be nuked there with AoE (or with a simple Bow as it is has effect of multi-hit)
It seems there is a hard limit of number of monster on one tile, but that is a no problem,  as soon monsters are killed, monster from outside will come there
You don't need to build signs on that area level, only where chaos stones position count for monster's level
so for map area with little land, like 3200, you can try this:

TTTTTTTTTTTTT
          TTTTTTTTTTTTT
          TTTTTTTTTTTTT
          TTXXXXTXXTTTT
          TTXXXX XXTTTT
          TTXXXX XXTTTT
          TTXXXXTXXTTTT    <= 3200 area
          TTTTTTTTTTTTT    <= other level area
          TTTTTTTTTTTTT
          TTTTTTTTTTTTT
other area ^^ 3200 area

column1: Gear and skills (standards, easy to get)

column2: 1) farming chaos stones:

column3: Gear: Bow (S/ Champion Bow from Equipment Gacha, A/Swift Bow from Saturday S rank event) due to its effect of multi-hit in closed space, Ninja HeadBand, Kairo shield, Purple Cape, Red ribbon

skills: Stubborn is a must, then 1 AoE skill (best with Myriad Arrow from farming Wairo easiest level, Area Attack is less efficient because Myriad Arrow is multihit AoE with less MP while Area Attack is only 1 hit AoE)
Exp I, II, III for 3.28 times Exp
Shield and Bow resistance if weakness

Nice to have: Perfect Dodge for survival, Full Reflect, Facility Rec Up MP (because full recovery in Bath check for full HP to stop using facility, so sometime MP isn't full yet when residents exit Recovery Facility if Recovery Facility has HP recovery better than MP recovery)
For defense vs raid during full moon, there are two main strategies:

- either build towns far from farms, e.g: my town at original position and another town at map level 70 doesn't get attacked at all by farm at 3200, it seems that the safe distance is 125
- or build a layer of lure around windmill, then another layer of wall around all that. All of these should be leveled up, and equip everyone to fight

A must is Stubborn skill, without it, that won't work
Another important skill to have is Myriad Arrow. Area attack would work but less efficient and costs 3 times MP of Myriad Arrow, so resident couldn't be lower level/awakening than having Myriad Arrow, 
Fighter set with MP priority (Ninja head, A/Swift Bow, Kairo shield, Purple Cape, Red Ribbon)
For reason of MP consumption of Stubborn, Kairo shield instead of Wairo shield, shield resistance if Weak, Facility MP recover UP
Another nice to have: Perfect dodge, Full Reflect, EXP I II III

column2: 2) Residents (dumb D labor or C from marriage of 2 D rank with A compatibility): I breed mostly D/ Monk with D/Champion => 1 awakening C/Champion or 1 awakening C/ Berserker due to their tanky stats

column3:
Due to damage calculation, 0 awakening D rank can hit 3200 monster with maximum 10 damage and they miss alot, but it can do 1200+ damage to 3200 monster with crit, so crit is important
5-6 D rank can beat in average 1 3200 monster

Everyone, except Mage, either fighter town or not, have fighter set (Ninja head, A/ rank weapons or C/Silver Scapel, Wairo/Kairo Shield, Purple Cape, Red Ribborn), then the must skill is Stubborn,  another nice to have is Move Spped Up, All Out Sprint, Facility MP Recovery UP
Have lots of Recovery Room (Monk room, due to its bigger recovery than Hospital), Rej Spring and Bath, because they will die alot once you farm 3200 level
Have several activity skill: mix of Construction Chief, Ranch Know How, Treasure Analysis, Agriculturist
If you have several choice of weapons from S/ daily rank rewards (Emerald Clubs, Sword ...) prefer sth with Luck, for crit purpose, speed is less important
Equip one of multihit skill so they can solo better vs high level monster (e.g: 5-hit skill)

column2: MP consumption: depends on average level, so no needs to increase 5-stat if you don't want to. The MP will vary between MP min and max (check the Skill tab), I didn't check if it increases linearly or follows a curve, but I doubt it is mostly linear
Chaos stones location:

column3:
There are 3 area for chaos stones (2 at each area for a total of 6 foundable from survey):
- volcano tile of at least level 45 (so at early game, you can find it on volcano tile at level 75)
- sand tile of at least level 60 (so you can begin to find it on first map extension, area 76 or 92)
- grass tile of at least level 90 (you need to wait till 3rd map extension, area 454, 777)

column2: Shining monster and eggs: every 30 monster of same type defeated will generate a shining monster of same type and 10 levels higher than the last defeated. Defeat it will generate eggs with ranks and levels random (formula provided above)

The chaos stones 's duration is 12 in-game months (1 in-game year). Once it reach the 13th months, it will need orange. It seems that 1 in-game day = 1 in-game month. Depends on lags, for one normal game where 1 in-game day is around 4 mins, 12 in-game month should be around 48 mins

Cave doesn't spawn any monster during night: The algorithm in game is that each night, they will begin by spawn chaos stones monster then they will try to spawn normal cave.
There is a check in normal cave spawn though, it first look up the maxEnemies column in Rank tab (higher your rank, the more monster can be spawned), if limit has been reached, normal cave won't spawn more enemies.
There is another particular point with normal cave is that it will try to spawn monster every 3 hour during night (so in around three waves), if you can kill monster to go under the limit before each check
